No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to poverty eradication or its specific targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to hunger, food security, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to health, well-being, or medical topics.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) focused on developing 'English written communication skills' and 'academic writing training' for 'business context' and 'international studies', directly supporting relevant skills for employment and further education (Target 4.4).

prepare themselves for more advanced academic work and international studies

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to energy access or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course prepares students for the 'business world' and 'business context' by developing 'English written communication skills', directly supporting decent job creation (Target 8.3) and full, productive employment (Target 8.5).

gives students an insight of the business world

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Topics like 'Product Development', 'Product Brochure Design', and 'Mini-Research Paper' contribute to fostering innovation and enhancing technological capabilities (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to reducing inequalities based on income, age, sex, disability, race, ethnicity, or other status.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to urban development, housing, or public spaces.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course emphasizes 'Respect Copyright' and 'Saying “No” to Plagiarism', and includes 'ethics' in core competencies, promoting rule of law (Target 16.3) and accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course prepares students for 'international studies as exchange students' and includes collaborative events like 'ETP Trade Show' and 'ETP Research Symposium', fostering skills relevant to international cooperation and partnerships (Target 17.9).
